title: China Labels Japanese JMSDF Warship Transit of Taiwan Strait a 'Provocation'
detail: China's military condemned the passage of Japan's JMSDF destroyer Ikazuchi through the Taiwan Strait — transiting from 4:02 a.m. to 5:50 p.m. local time — calling it 'a deliberate provocation' that threatened regional peace. This was the first Japanese destroyer transit since Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi took office. The vessel subsequently headed to the South China Sea to participate in US-Philippines Balikatan joint exercises. The transit marks a broadening of freedom-of-navigation operations in the strait beyond US-led missions to include Japanese naval forces.

media.caption: Chinese military labels Japanese warship transit of Taiwan Strait a provocation

title: President Lai Inspects Army Training Command, Urges Immediate Passage of NT$1.25T Defense Budget
detail: President Lai Ching-te inspected the Army Infantry Training Command's Jintang Camp in Kaohsiung, calling for swift passage of the NT$1.25 trillion special defense budget. Lai stated 'Only through constant preparedness can threats be deterred,' characterizing the budget as both necessary and affordable. The presidential inspection came one day after Taiwan's legislature agreed to end a 230-day budget deadlock and begin deliberations. US AIT Director Raymond Greene has previously called on all parties to pass the special defense budget without further delay.

media.caption: President Lai Ching-te inspects Jintang Army Training Camp and urges passage of NT$1.25 trillion defense budget
